summary: Full-stack testing done. Backend: 20/20 pytest tests pass (health, lead CRUD, admin auth, admin leads CRUD/stats, validation, ObjectId leakage). Frontend: home, services, gallery, contact submission, admin login/dashboard/status-update/logout all working. Resend integration confirmed via backend log 'Lead email sent: <id>'. One HIGH-priority frontend bug: 'Book Now' on a service card (Home + Services pages) navigates to /contact but does NOT pre-select the service on the Contact form (form.service stays empty).

ui_bugs.issue: Service pre-selection from ServicesPage/HomePage 'Book Now' does not work. window.history.state.usr contains {service: '<title>'} after navigation, but ContactPage's useEffect on location.state does not set form.service — Select trigger still shows placeholder 'Choose a service'. Confirmed on both /services (services-page-book-<key>) and Home (service-book-<key>) via multiple clicks (kitchen, residential).

action_items: Fix ContactPage service pre-selection: verify useLocation().state is being read on mount in React 19 + react-router-dom v7. As a robust fix, also read from window.history.state?.usr as a fallback, or pass the service via query param (?service=...) which survives remounts/StrictMode. The Radix Select value binding itself is fine — the issue is that form.service never receives location.state.service.

Optional: The frontend test-credentials memory file said RESEND_API_KEY was empty, but it is actually set in backend/.env and emails ARE being delivered (verified in backend.err.log: 'Lead email sent: <id>'). Update /app/memory/test_credentials.md to reflect the current live state.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py `send_lead_email` is awaited inside the request handler; if Resend is slow the client waits. Consider fire-and-forget via asyncio.create_task so the client always sees fast success. (Currently latency is acceptable.)

server.py CORS: allow_credentials=True combined with allow_origins=['*'] is browser-invalid. Since only a bearer token is used (no cookies from the frontend), this is not exploited today, but should be tightened to the actual frontend origin.
server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown'); should migrate to lifespan handlers.
ContactPage — pre-selection from cross-page navigation is fragile (relies on router state that gets lost on refresh). Prefer URL query param (?service=<slug>) for shareability and reliability.
AdminDashboardPage stores JWT in localStorage — vulnerable to XSS token theft. Backend already supports httpOnly cookies; consider migrating.
seed_admin uses ADMIN_EMAIL/ADMIN_PASSWORD env; behavior is idempotent (updates password if changed). Good.
seed_data_creation: Created a few 'TEST_' prefixed leads via POST /api/leads and one via UI (playwright.test@example.com) — safe to leave; used TEST_ prefix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All CRUD + auth flows are verified. Only outstanding functional issue is service pre-selection on /contact when navigating from /services or / Home 'Book Now' cards. Backend fully green; use /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py for regression. Resend email verified working via backend.err.log line 'Lead email sent: <id>'.
rca of the issue: history.state.usr = {service: 'Kitchen & Appliance Cleaning'} is correctly written by react-router's useNavigate(_, { state }). However, ContactPage's useEffect(() => { if (location.state?.service) setForm(...); }, [location.state]) does not update form.service — verified by opening the Select: all 8 options show aria-selected='false' and data-state='unchecked'. Likely cause: StrictMode double-mount + React 19 useLocation returning the state only on the discarded first render, or a race between initial state hydration and Layout's Outlet remount. Recommended mitigation: pass service via query param (navigate(`/contact?service=${encodeURIComponent(s.title)}`)) and read new URLSearchParams(location.search).get('service') in ContactPage useEffect — this is deterministic and survives refresh.
